invariants: Tenant isolation is the top property: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL; every query filters by workspace_id.
Service-role Lambda code bypasses RLS — it MUST scope by workspace_id in code; RLS is only the backstop for user-context (admin app) reads.
workspace_id is NEVER taken from a client payload — derive it from the API key (ingest) or the authorizer-injected claim (admin API).
Per-profile ordering via MessageGroupId=profile_id; code must be idempotent and order-convergent (progress-before-profile_created still converges).
Idempotency: events.event_id is the dedupe key (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING).
Segment rule-AST -> SQL is compiled with a field/operator whitelist, parameterized SQL only, and ALWAYS prepends workspace_id = $ws. Never interpolate raw input.
Local-only: verify against LocalStack + Supabase CLI/Testcontainers. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier. Mock SES (never send real mail).
Scope discipline: implement exactly what the cited spec sections require — no extra features, no gold-plating.

criteria: A Workspace-A JWT cannot read/modify Workspace-B rows under RLS; service-role code paths still scope by workspace_id in code.
Two workspaces with the same external_id/email keep fully separate profiles (UNIQUE(workspace_id, external_id)).
Roles (§3A): marketer cannot manage users/domains/billing; accounting can read billing but cannot edit segments/automations; owner can do both within the workspace.
system-admin can view across companies and every cross-tenant access is recorded in admin_audit_log.
Multi-workspace switching: a user in two workspaces sees only the active workspace; switching changes the workspace_id claim and re-scopes; no cross-bleed.
scope: packages/db: workspaces, workspace_users, platform_admins, admin_audit_log, workspace_api_keys tables + RLS policies (workspace_id = auth.jwt()->>workspace_id) with the narrow system-admin (is_platform_admin) exception.
packages/tenancy: workspace-context + role-check helpers (owner/marketer/accounting capability matrix from §3A).

services/authorizer: Lambda authorizer that validates a Supabase JWT (JWKS), resolves workspace membership + role (or is_platform_admin), and injects workspace_id + role into request context. Logic in a pure injected function.
phase.specSections: §3 (multi-tenancy + RLS caveat), §3A (4-role model), §6 (workspaces, workspace_users, platform_admins, admin_audit_log, workspace_api_keys), §12 (role-aware API), §13 (security)
phase.title: Auth + authorizer + roles

implUnits: 1. packages/db: verify 0002+0006 apply; add test-only non-BYPASSRLS app role + session-claim helper
7. services/authorizer: thin handler with jose JWKS + lookups
plan.notes: §6 tenancy tables (0002) + RLS (0006) ALREADY EXIST with FORCE RLS, uniform tenant_isolation policy, narrow OR app_is_platform_admin() exception, platform_only policies, workspace_users self-membership carve-out for switcher. profiles UNIQUE(workspace_id,external_id) in 0003. RLS helpers read current_setting('request.jwt.claims',true)::jsonb -> integration tests set claim via set_config, not real JWT. CRITICAL: run RLS integration tests as non-superuser WITHOUT BYPASSRLS or they pass vacuously; use separate service-role connection to seed and to prove bypass. Mock JWKS/jose + AWS SDK; never mock Postgres. Add jose dep to authorizer, pg/testcontainers to db tests.

assertions: connect as non-superuser non-BYPASSRLS app role so FORCE RLS applies
set request.jwt.claims via set_config and assert app_current_workspace_id()
INSERT with wsB under wsA claim rejected by WITH CHECK
switch claim to wsB, symmetric result
service-role connection bypasses RLS (sees both workspaces)
scopedQuery helper always emits workspace_id=$n bound to wsA
returns only wsA rows despite bypass-capable connection
same external_id in wsA and wsB both succeed
second same external_id in wsA fails 23505
owner all within workspace not view_all_workspaces
accounting denied segment/automation edit, allowed billing read
active workspace not in memberships and not admin => Deny
is_platform_admin claim reads wsB tenant table
admin path writes one row, normal read writes none
re-issue claim wsB on same connection returns only wsB
user sees own membership rows for both

previousGateFeedback: Disable turbo caching for DB-integration tests: add cache:false to the test task (or proper inputs incl live DB). Initial green run was a cache replay masking a hard failure.

Make ensureTestAppRole() concurrency-safe OR run db integration files serially (vitest fileParallelism:false / singleThread), OR do the ALTER ROLE/GRANT DDL once in global setup under pg_advisory_xact_lock. Root cause of 'tuple concurrently updated' is parallel ALTER ROLE/GRANT on shared catalog tuples.
Give each integration file a unique fixture namespace; avoid broad DELETE FROM workspaces in beforeAll that races other files.
Add CI step running pnpm test with cleared cache (turbo --force) so the merge gate can't pass on stale cache.

instructions: Read CDP-BUILD-SPEC.md sections: §3 (multi-tenancy + RLS caveat), §3A (4-role model), §6 (workspaces, workspace_users, platform_admins, admin_audit_log, workspace_api_keys), §12 (role-aware API), §13 (security).
Address the previousGateFeedback precisely; adjust tests/impl as needed.
Honor every invariant in context.invariants — tenant isolation and the workspace_id rules are pass/fail.
Integration tests MUST run against a real local Postgres (Supabase CLI/Testcontainers); do NOT mock the DB. Mock SES and AWS SDK (aws-sdk-client-mock).
Run `pnpm test` (the relevant package/filter), `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint`. Iterate until tests are GREEN. Report the actual command output summary.
Implement ONLY what the cited sections require — no out-of-scope features.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { filesCreated: string[], filesModified: string[], testsPass: boolean, testSummary: string, typecheckOk: boolean, lintOk: boolean, commandsRun: string[], notes: string }
prompt.role: senior TypeScript engineer building a serverless multi-tenant CDP, test-first
prompt.task: Implement "Auth + authorizer + roles" to satisfy its test plan and §18 criteria. Red -> green -> refactor.
